title: Star Wars: Fall of the Empire
code_by: Raymond Davidson / Andrew Wilkening / Mike Vinikour
description: The third Stern Star Wars based on the original trilogy. A classic 'fan' layout with tight shots that reward controlled play. Going to be everywhere for the next few years — learn it now.

summary.text: Star Wars: FotE has four separate systems all running at once, which is why it feels overwhelming at first. But here's the secret: you don't need to chase all four. Pick one or two per ball and you'll outscore most of your league. The FORCE targets (five standup targets scattered around the playfield — spell F-O-R-C-E by hitting them) are the engine that lights character shots for Rebel Missions. There are two main multiballs: Battle of Hoth (lock 3 balls at the Luke shot on the left) and Death Star Multiball (bash the Death Star sculpt to lock 3 balls). The pop bumpers quietly build shot multipliers that can double or triple any shot you make. The insert color at each character shot tells you the mission difficulty: yellow = easy, green = medium, blue = hard. And the center ramp is the easiest shot on the game — when in doubt, shoot that.

one_shot.name: Center Ramp
one_shot.description: Travis from The Pinball Company puts it simply: 'You can actually play this game and pretty much just focus on the middle and that will get you a lot of places.' The center ramp feeds back to the left flipper, advances the Falcon when the hyperdrive is lit, scores Han's character missions, starts Lightspeed Multiball, and can be hit reliably from either flipper at almost any speed. On the Premium/LE, the ramp physically raises and lowers — when it's down, hit the right ramp or the back of the center shot to raise it, then spam it. When you don't know what to do, shoot the center ramp. When you're in a multiball and losing control, shoot the center ramp. It's your bailout, your bread-and-butter, and your best friend.

hard_box.text: Four simultaneous progression systems (FORCE targets, Falcon, Jabba, Vader) plus two multiball paths plus shot multipliers. It's a lot. The Darth Vader scoop on the left ejects fast and feeds awkwardly to the right flipper — drop catching helps if you've got the skill, but most of us are just going to flip and pray. The ion cannon bumpers near the slingshots make recovery harder when the ball is out of control, but they're also how you earn shot multipliers, so they're a necessary evil. The good news: the center ramp is very forgiving and feeds nicely from either flipper.

priorities.title: Use Ball Save to Hit FORCE Targets + Qualify Falcon
priorities.description: Every shot is lit for a skill shot on plunge — hit anything within a couple switch hits for a super skill shot (6M). Then use your ball save window to hit FORCE standup targets (the five targets with F-O-R-C-E letters near the major shots). You're doing two things at once: spelling FORCE to light rebel missions, and on the Premium/LE, hitting the right ramp or center shot to raise the center ramp for Falcon qualification. Don't worry about precision here — any FORCE letter counts and the ball save protects you.

priorities.title: Spell F-O-R-C-E to Light Character Shots
priorities.description: Hit the five FORCE standup targets scattered around the playfield — they're the small targets near each major shot with letters on them. On default settings, hitting three targets spots the remaining letters for you, so you don't need to find all five. Once FORCE is complete, all 8 character shots light up with colored inserts. The color tells you the difficulty: yellow = first mission (easy), green = second (medium), blue = third (hard). Now you can pick which character's mission to start by shooting their lit shot.

priorities.title: Start a Rebel Mission — Pick Han (Center Ramp)
priorities.description: With character shots lit, shoot the center ramp to start a Han mission. Han's modes use hurry-up timers that start high, and the center ramp is the easiest shot on the game — it's the natural first choice. During the mission, blue shots advance you through it and white shots build the jackpot value for the next blue shot. Rapidly flashing blue shots are worth 2x. Completing the mission is important: Han's next mission will be worth 2x, which compounds into serious points on Ball 2.

priorities.title: Feed the Center Ramp to Advance the Falcon
priorities.description: When the blue hyperdrive light under the Falcon model is lit, center ramp shots count toward starting a Falcon Feature. The first feature is Lightspeed Multiball — shoot yellow shots to build jackpot value, then blue shots during the multiball for jackpots, and loop the center ramp for repeatable super jackpots. This happens naturally if you're already shooting the center ramp for Han missions.

priorities.title: Start Lightspeed or Battle of Hoth Multiball
priorities.description: You have two paths here depending on what you built on Ball 1. Lightspeed: if you advanced the Falcon, hit the center ramp when lit to start the multiball. Battle of Hoth: hit the Light Lock target (the droids target below the AT-AT), then lock 3 balls at the Luke shot (the dead-end VUK on the left that feeds back to your left flipper). Both are valid openers — center ramp spam leads to Lightspeed, while deliberate lock-building leads to Hoth. Pick whichever feels more natural based on what you built on Ball 1.

priorities.title: In Any Multiball: Sarlacc Pit First for Add-a-Ball
priorities.description: The moment multiball starts, your first priority is the Sarlacc pit (Chewie's whirlpool ramp on the right — knock down the drop target in front of it, then shoot the ramp). The ball spins around the pit accumulating spins. Once you hit 10 spins, the action button lights for add-a-ball. Do this first in every multiball — an extra ball is worth more than any individual jackpot.

priorities.title: Use Your Shot Multipliers
priorities.description: The ion cannon pop bumpers have been quietly building a shot multiplier for you. When the rebel inserts at a major shot start pulsing, the next shot there is worth 2x. If you see a pulsing insert near a shot you're about to make anyway — a jackpot shot, a mode completion — time it. The multiplier becomes solid (permanent 2x) once you hit the pulsing shot.

priorities.title: Start a Second Mission — Han Is Worth 2x Now
priorities.description: If you completed Han's first mission on Ball 1, his next mission is worth 2x. That's a significant scoring bump from just doing the same thing again. Spell FORCE again (harder this time — you'll need to hit more targets) and shoot the center ramp for another Han mission. The 2x value stacks with shot multipliers for serious points.

priorities.title: Start Death Star Multiball If Hoth Is Done
priorities.description: Shoot the Death Star ramp to lower the target, then lock 3 balls behind it. During the multiball: shoot red shots for jackpots (can't repeat the same shot), and after 15 jackpots the Death Star opens for a super jackpot. This is your second big scoring opportunity if Hoth already happened.

priorities.title: Collect Stormtrooper Hurry-Ups
priorities.description: That hidden standup target on the right has been counting Stormtroopers all game. If the hurry-up is lit, cash it in — each one adds to your bonus multiplier AND increases the value of the next one. Free points if you notice it.

priorities.title: Rip the Spinners for JEDI Ball Save
priorities.description: Spinner shots at either orbit light letters in J-E-D-I. Complete it and you've earned a ball save activated by the action button — basically a get-out-of-jail-free card. On Ball 3, this is survival insurance. Use the Force. Literally.

items.title: JEDI Ball Save
items.description: Spell J-E-D-I by hitting the orbit shots (both sides) and spinner shots — watch the upper-right corner of the LCD where the letters fill in. Each orbit or spinner shot adds progress. Once JEDI is complete, press the action button when the ball is heading for a drain. On the Premium/LE, the action button lets you control which direction the ball goes during the save. Work toward this every ball — a couple orbit shots per ball adds up.

items.title: Center Ramp Bailout
items.description: When things go sideways, the center ramp feeds safely to the left flipper from either side. Use it to reset and think.

items.title: Vader Scoop Feeds
items.description: The left scoop ejects fast toward the right flipper. Be ready. If you can drop catch, great. If not, just flip toward the right ramp or right orbit — both are reasonable bailout shots from that feed.

items.title: Bottom Lanes (1-2-3-4)
items.description: Cycle with flippers. Completing them advances bonus multiplier and eventually lights Vader modes and mystery. They complete themselves if you just remember to cycle.

items.title: Nudging Matters Here
items.description: No built-in outlane ball saves on this game — you earn the JEDI save. Nudging is more important than on most modern Sterns.

items.title: Vader Modes early
items.description: They require completing bottom lanes, then shooting the left scoop, then hitting enough shots during a timed mode. A lot of steps for inconsistent payoff at our level. Let them happen naturally.

items.title: Jabba Events on Ball 1 (except Rancor)
items.description: The Sarlacc Pit drop target is in a dangerous spot near the flippers — you have to knock it down twice to qualify a Jabba event. On Ball 1 without a ball save, that's risky. The exception: if you happen to qualify it naturally, pick Rancor (use the action button to select it as the ball spins in the pit). Rancor is a combo mode with no timer where you hit orange shots in sequence — it can score over 100M and doesn't punish slow play.

items.title: Chasing Hyperspace Loops
items.description: They require enough TIE fighters from the right ramp, then looping the center ramp repeatedly. Cool when it happens, but don't build your strategy around it.

items.title: 'Not a Jedi Yet' wizard mode
items.description: Requires starting missions at 4 unique characters. If it lights, shoot it. But don't twist yourself into knots trying to start missions at difficult characters just to qualify it.

between_us.label: Good Scores to Aim For
between_us.text: 300M+ wins a lot of tournament games on this. If you break 500M you're having a great night. Billion-point games are possible but require mode stacking and shot multiplier timing beyond what we're covering here. Consistency at 300M beats one 600M game followed by two house balls — and that's the whole philosophy of this site.

between_us.label: Pro vs. Premium vs. LE
between_us.text: The big difference for strategy: on the Pro, the center ramp is a simple fixed jump ramp. On Premium/LE, it physically raises and lowers — when lowered, it becomes a target. Right ramp shots or center ramp hits raise it back up. This means on Premium/LE you occasionally can't shoot the center ramp when you expect to. The core strategy (spell FORCE, start missions, get into multiball, use multipliers) is the same across all models.

playfield_callouts.name: Center Ramp
playfield_callouts.description: The easiest and most important shot on the game. Feeds left flipper, advances Falcon, starts Lightspeed, scores Han missions. Your bailout from either flipper.

playfield_callouts.name: FORCE Standup Targets
playfield_callouts.description: Five targets scattered across the playfield. Spelling F-O-R-C-E lights all character shots for Rebel Missions. The engine of the game.

playfield_callouts.name: Luke VUK / AT-AT Shot
playfield_callouts.description: Left side VUK that spirals around the AT-AT model. Locks balls for Battle of Hoth multiball. Can be hit from either flipper with precision.

playfield_callouts.name: Death Star Ramp
playfield_callouts.description: Left side ramp with a target at the apex. Lower the target to reveal the ball lock behind it. Lock 3 balls for Death Star Multiball. Also Lando's shot during missions.

playfield_callouts.name: Sarlacc Pit
playfield_callouts.description: Chewie's shot. Whirlpool ramp where each spin changes the mode selection. Guarded by Salacious Crumb (drop target). Best hit as a backhand from the right flipper. During multiball, 10 spins here lights action button add-a-ball.

sources.title: Travis / The Pinball Company — SW:FotE Basic Strategies Video (Transcript)
sources.description: Two complete strategy demonstrations on LE model. Key insights: center ramp strategy as primary opener, Battle of Hoth as alternative, Sarlacc pit add-a-ball priority in all multiballs, Rancor as best Jabba event (100M+), JEDI ball save qualification via orbits/spinners, Han mission 2x value after completion, mission color coding (yellow/green/cyan).

sources.title: Kineticist — 'May the Force Be With You' Tutorial
sources.description: Primary source. Noah Crable's comprehensive written tutorial covering character perks, mode details, multiball mechanics, Jabba events, and tournament strategies.

chat_starters: Which character mission should I start with?
What's the easiest way to start a multiball?
What's the deal with the FORCE targets?

level_up.intro: Ready to push further? These strategies involve stacking modes and multiballs — the stuff we deliberately left out of the main ball plans because it requires more ball control and game knowledge. Come back here once the basic ball plans feel automatic.

tips.title: Stack Lightspeed + Hoth
tips.description: Start Lightspeed Escape before collecting your 3rd Hoth lock. This lets both multiballs run simultaneously — the only legal stack in the game. Build Lightspeed jackpots first, then lock the 3rd ball during Lightspeed for maximum chaos.

tips.title: Jabba's Palace + Multiball (conceptual)
tips.description: Jabba's Palace has unlimited scoring potential as long as you keep hitting the drop target to reset the timer. In theory, stacking this with a multiball would be enormous — but multiballs can't stack with Jabba events. Instead, build Jabba's Palace value during single-ball play and use the Rancor combo mode for its no-timer advantage.

tips.title: Shot Multiplier Timing
tips.description: The ion cannon bumpers build your shot multiplier. When it's ready, the NEXT shot you make gets 2x-3x. If you can trap the ball and wait for the right moment — like right before a super jackpot collect or a mode completion shot — you'll dramatically increase your score. This is the single biggest scoring lever for intermediate players moving to advanced.
